title: Ivan Pesic and His World Tour

content.problemStatement:
Chef is very popular, so his friends visit him often. Chef's friend Pesic decided to come visit him in Chefland during one of Pesic's famous world tours, but he got lost along the way and ended up in Queryland. Queryland has a weird structure: it has NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) and N−1N-1N−1 bidirectional roads connecting them in such a way that it is possible to reach any city in Queryland from any other city.

Pesic visited all cities in Queryland and assigned values to them according to his experiences. Let's denote the initial value of city iii by AiA_iAi​. Now that Pesic has seen all cities, he wants to play in Queryland for a bit longer. He has QQQ queries of two types:


`1 X Y`: Consider the shortest path between cities XXX and YYY. Let's denote the length (number of cities, including the endpoints) of this path by LLL. Check whether the values of cities on this path (including the endpoints) form a permutation of the integers 111 through LLL.
`2 X Z`: Change the value of city XXX to ZZZ.
You are a big fan of Pesic, so he lets you process these queries for him.
constraints: 1≤Ai≤N1 \le A_i \le N1≤Ai​≤N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500,000500,000500,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 500,000500,000500,000
**Subtask #2 (40 points):** v=u+1v = u+1v=u+1 for each road
Query 1: The values of cities on the path between cities 444 and 333 are 444, 222, 111 and 333, so they form a permutation of 1,2,3,41, 2, 3, 41,2,3,4.
Query 2: The values of cities on the path between cities 101010 and 333 are 101010, 444, 222, 111 and 333, so they do not form a permutation of 1,2,3,4,51, 2, 3, 4, 51,2,3,4,5.
Query 3: The value of city 101010 changes from 101010 to 555.
Query 4: The values of cities on the path between cities 101010 and 333 are now 555, 444, 222, 111 and 333, which is a permutation of 1,2,3,4,51, 2, 3, 4, 51,2,3,4,5.
Query 5: The values of cities on the path between cities 555 and 333 are 555, 222, 111 and 333, which is not a permutation of 1,2,3,41, 2, 3, 41,2,3,4.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that cities uuu and vvv are connected by a road.
The following QQQ lines describe queries.

Each of these lines starts with an integer ttt denoting the type of the current query: t=1t = 1t=1 for a query of the first type or t=2t = 2t=2 for a query of the second type.
If t=1t = 1t=1, it is followed by a space and two space-separated integers XXX and YYY describing a query of the first type.
Otherwise, it is followed by a space and two space-separated integers XXX and ZZZ describing a query of the second type.
content.outputFormat: For each query of the first type, print a single line containing the string `"Yes"` if the values on the given path are a correct permutation or `"No"` if they are not.
